title: Savory Gooey Filipino Beef Bistek
description: A Filipino dish featuring tender beef, gooey liquid cheese, and a savory sauce, all steamed to perfection in a Buddhist-temple-friendly recipe. This fermented beef bistek is a unique twist on traditional Filipino cuisine.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, calamansi juice, black pepper, and salt to make the marinade.
Add the beef strips to the marinade and mix well to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat and add the beef strips, cooking until browned on all sides.
Remove the beef from the skillet and set aside, then add the sliced onion and cook until softened.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the liquid cheese to the skillet and stir until melted and smooth.
Return the beef strips to the skillet and stir to coat with the cheese sauce.
Add the dried bay leaves and water to the skillet, then bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 10 minutes, until the sauce has thickened and the beef is tender.
Serve the bistek hot, garnished with additional calamansi juice and black pepper if desired.
